popularStory.headline: Robert de Cassel
popularStory.description:
Robert de Cassel (né vers 1278, mort en 1331), seigneur de Cassel, Dunkerque, Gravelines, deuxième fils de Robert III de Flandre et de Yolande de Bourgogne comtesse de Nevers.
En 1318, Robert reçoit e …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Of Flanders,  Count of Marle於1275年出生在法國諾門迪瑟-馬利達皮-賽吉尼可拉斯時，他的父親Robert de Béthune III Graaf van Vlaanderen26歲，母親Yolande de Bourgogne II of Nevers28歲。他於1323年9月21日與Jeanne De Bretagne Princess of Brittany結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1331年5月26日在法國諾爾德瓦內頓享年56歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert Of Flanders,  Count of Marle于1275年出生在法国諾門迪瑟-馬利達皮-賽吉尼可拉斯时，他的父亲Robert de Béthune III Graaf van Vlaanderen26岁，母亲Yolande de Bourgogne II of Nevers28岁。他于1323年9月21日与Jeanne De Bretagne Princess of Brittany结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1331年5月26日在法国諾爾德瓦內頓享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Flanders,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: ethnic name from Middle English Fla(u)ndres ‘Flanders’, denoting someone from Flanders, a historical region covering parts of modern-day Belgium, France, and the Netherlands. Compare Fleming .

variants.name: Fleming
meanings: English: ethnic name for someone from Flanders, from Middle English fleming. The word reflects a Norman French form of Old French flamanc ‘Fleming’, from the stem flam- + the ancient Germanic suffix -ing. In the Middle Ages there was considerable commercial intercourse between England and the Netherlands, particularly in the wool trade, and many Flemish weavers and dyers settled in England. This surname is also common in south and east Scotland and in Ireland, where it is sometimes found in the Gaelicized form Pléamonn.
German: variant of   Flemming  , cognate with 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Count
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French counte ‘count’ (Old French conte, from Latin comes, comitem); a title of French and Norman nobility, ranking as equivalent to English Earl .

variants.name: Earl
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English e(o)rl ‘earl’, a traditional English title of nobility equivalent in rank to French and Norman Count .

variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Marle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Middle English marle ‘marl’ (Old French marle), i.e. clay mixed with calcium carbonate, often spread as a fertilizer on sandy soils. Compare Marler .

variants.name: Marl
meanings: English:  see  Marle  .

variants.name: Marler
meanings: occupational name from Middle English marler, either ‘spreader of marl (as fertilizer)’ or ‘hewer or quarrier of marl’.
topographic name from Middle English marlere ‘marl-pit’ (Old French marliere).
in Norfolk, perhaps also a variant of   Morley  .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Of Flanders,  Count of Marle was born in 1275, in Dampierre-Saint-Nicolas, Seine-Maritime, Normandy, France, his father, Robert de Béthune III Graaf van Vlaanderen, was 26 and his mother, Yolande de Bourgogne II of Nevers, was 28. He married Jeanne De Bretagne Princess of Brittany on 21 September 1323. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 26 May 1331, in Warneton, Nord, France, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Of Flanders,  Count of Marle est né en 1275, à Dampierre-Saint-Nicolas, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, France, son père, Robert de Béthune III Graaf van Vlaanderen, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Yolande de Bourgogne II of Nevers, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Jeanne De Bretagne Princess of Brittany le 21 septembre 1323. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 26 mai 1331, à Warneton, Nord, France, à l’âge de 56 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert Of Flanders,  Count of Marle 1275 in Dampierre-Saint-Nicolas, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert de Béthune III Graaf van Vlaanderen 26 und seine Mutter Yolande de Bourgogne II of Nevers 28. Er hat am 21. September 1323 Jeanne De Bretagne Princess of Brittany geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 26. Mai 1331 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Warneton, Nord, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Of Flanders,  Count of Marle è nato il nel 1275 nel Dampierre-Saint-Nicolas, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, Robert de Béthune III Graaf van Vlaanderen, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Yolande de Bourgogne II of Nevers, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Jeanne De Bretagne Princess of Brittany il 21 settembre 1323. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 26 maggio 1331, nel Warneton, Nord, Francia, all'età di 56 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert Of Flanders,  Count of Marleが1275年にフランスノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメダンパエレ-サイント-ニコラスで生まれた時，父Robert de Béthune III Graaf van Vlaanderenは26歳，母Yolande de Bourgogne II of Neversは28歳であった。1323年9月21日にJeanne De Bretagne Princess of Brittanyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1331年5月26日にフランスノードワーネトンで56歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Of Flanders,  Count of Marle(이)가 1275년 프랑스 노먼디 세이인-마이팀 댐피레-세인트-니콜라스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Béthune III Graaf van Vlaanderen(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Yolande de Bourgogne II of Nevers(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1323년 9월 21일 Jeanne De Bretagne Princess of Brittany(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1331년 5월 26일 프랑스 노르드 워네턴에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Of Flanders,  Count of Marle nasceu em 1275, em Dampierre-Saint-Nicolas, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, França, seu pai, Robert de Béthune III Graaf van Vlaanderen, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Yolande de Bourgogne II of Nevers, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Jeanne De Bretagne Princess of Brittany em 21 de setembro de 1323. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 26 de maio de 1331, em Warneton, Nord, França, com 56 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Of Flanders,  Count of Marle родился в 1275 г. в местности дампьирре-сеинт-николас, сине-маритим, норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Robert de Béthune III Graaf van Vlaanderen было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Yolande de Bourgogne II of Nevers, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Jeanne De Bretagne Princess of Brittany 21 сентября 1323 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 26 мая 1331 г. в местности варнетон, норд, Франция в возрасте 56 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert Of Flanders,  Count of Marle nació en 1275, en Dampierre-Saint-Nicolas, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia, su papá, Robert de Béthune III Graaf van Vlaanderen, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Yolande de Bourgogne II of Nevers, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Jeanne De Bretagne Princess of Brittany el 21 de septiembre de 1323. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 26 de mayo de 1331, en Warneton, Nord, Francia, a la edad de 56 años.
